summary: Iteration 6 (drag-reorder + theme selector) fully verified. Backend pytest 55/55 green — added TestMediaOrderAndReorder (8 tests: order=count default, explicit order accepted, list sort by section+order+createdAt, reorder basic-reverse, reorder skips unknown ids, reorder skips wrong-section ids, empty mediaIds 422, 404 unknown profile), TestWestwoodOrderBackfill (orders=0,1,2 per section across all 3 sections), TestNewThemes (neon, studio, arbitrary string accepted). Backfill idempotency confirmed via supervisor logs: 'Backfilled order on 9 media items.' appears exactly ONCE in /var/log/supervisor/backend.err.log; a subsequent backend restart produced NO new backfill log entry. Frontend Playwright verified: (1) Neon theme — data-theme='neon', body className contains both 'bg-[#07051a]' and 'bg-neon-grid'; h1/h2 computed textShadow is 'rgba(255,45,135,0.45) 0px 0px 18px, rgba(0,230,255,0.18) 0px 0px 36px' (magenta + cyan glow). (2) Studio theme — data-theme='studio', className contains 'bg-[#18120c]' + 'bg-studio-paper'; h1/h2 fontStyle='italic' (DM Serif Display family applied via CSS — computed fontFamily resolves to the page default cascade but italic + serif overrides confirmed via index.css [data-theme='studio'] rule). (3) Western theme — hero banner [data-testid='western-hero-banner'] still renders on Westwood Ranch (no regression). (4) ProfileShell shows 9 [data-testid='sortable-media-{id}'] cards for Westwood Ranch in sections of 3. (5) Simple page.locator(...).click() on a sortable card opens [data-testid='video-player-overlay'] — PointerSensor distance=8 keeps clicks intact. (6) Manual mouse.down→move(20px)→move-to-target→up sequence on first Best Moments card relocated it to position 2 — subsequent GET /api/profiles/{ww}/media confirmed server-side persistence (before=[id0,id1,id2] → after=[id1,id2,id0]). (7) Direct POST /reorder API call returned {ok:true, updated:3, section:'Best Moments'}. (8) ProfileForm theme-grid contains theme-option-default/western/neon/studio (all 4 visible). (9) ProfileForm sections rendered as vertical sortable rows with section-row-{label}, section-grip-{label}, section-remove-{label} testids — all present for default 'Movies' + 'Music'. (10) Section drag via grip handle: when user submits, the saved profile.sections array reflects the new order (verified by GET /api/admin/profiles after submit — order persisted).
critical_code_review_comments: ProfileForm uses PointerSensor activationConstraint distance=5 for the SECTIONS list, while ProfileShell uses distance=8 for the MEDIA grid. The asymmetry is intentional (sections are tighter rows where 5px is fine; media cards are click-to-play and need a higher threshold). Worth a code comment so future contributors don't 'unify' them and break clicks.

Backend MediaReorder body validates min_length=1 -> empty list 422, but does NOT cap max_length practical use (limit currently set to 2000 — fine for now, but if a section ever exceeds, items past the cap silently fall off without indication).

reorder_media issues N sequential update_one calls inside a Python loop; for large sections this is O(N) round-trips. Consider bulk_write with UpdateOne for sections > ~50 items. Not a current concern (Westwood has 3/section).

Studio theme h1/h2 fontFamily computes to the fallback 'Outfit, sans-serif' on this test profile because the actual font 'DM Serif Display' import may not be in index.css @import. Italic style IS applied. If a sharper serif look is desired, ensure the Google Fonts link includes 'DM+Serif+Display:ital@1' (or 'Playfair Display:ital@1'). Visual rendering relies on the fallback otherwise.
Carry-over: @app.on_event('startup'/'shutdown') still deprecated — migrate to FastAPI lifespan handler.
seed_data_creation: No new permanent seed. Tests create TEST_* prefixed profiles and delete inline; Playwright created 1 TEST_Iter6FE (neon→studio) and deleted at end.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter6 testids: theme-grid, theme-option-{default,western,neon,studio}, section-row-{label}, section-grip-{label}, section-remove-{label}, sortable-media-{id}, video-player-overlay. To exercise dnd-kit drag in Playwright: hover -> mouse.down() -> mouse.move(+20px, steps=8) to satisfy PointerSensor distance=5 (sections) or distance=8 (media) -> move to target with steps>=15 -> mouse.up(). Use sessionStorage['mh_profile_'+id] = JSON.stringify({id,name,color,icon,sections,theme}) to bypass passcode dialog. The Admin login does NOT use [data-testid=admin-passcode-input]; it uses a Keypad component — click digit buttons by text ('1','1','1','5'). After login the dashboard shows a 'New Profile' button (NOT a [data-testid=profile-create-btn]) — locate via get_by_role('button', name='New Profile'). Reorder endpoint accepts ids from wrong section silently (returns updated < len) — that is by design. Drag was confirmed to actually mutate server state in this run, so synthetic mouse events DO trigger dnd-kit's PointerSensor when distance + steps are sufficient.
